C:\Users\DELL\AppData\Local\Temp\ipykernel_13172\3231387223.py:1: FutureWarning: A value is trying to be set on a copy of a DataFrame or Series through chained assignment using an inplace method.
The behavior will change in pandas 3.0. This inplace method will never work because the intermediate object on which we are setting values always behaves as a copy.

For example, when doing 'df[col].method(value, inplace=True)', try using 'df.method({col: value}, inplace=True)' or df[col] = df[col].method(value) instead, to perform the operation inplace on the original object.


  sales['promotion-ids'].fillna('no', inplace=True)

In [ ]:
## Data Visulations
## sales by categorty
In [56]:
sales['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(sales['Date'])
In [58]:
## CReate a revenue column
sales['Revenue'] = sales['Qty'] * sales['Amount']

STEP2 : Monthly Revenue¶

In [15]:
## Extract month-year
sales['Month'] = pd.to_datetime(sales['Date']).dt.to_period('M')
In [17]:
## Group by month
# STEP 1: Create Revenue column
sales['Revenue'] = sales['Qty'] * sales['Amount']

# STEP 2: Extract Month from Date
sales['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(sales['Date'])  # Ensure Date is in datetime format
sales['Month'] = sales['Date'].dt.to_period('M')  # Extract month as Period

# STEP 3: Group by Month and summarize Revenue
monthly_revenue = sales.groupby('Month')['Revenue'].sum().reset_index()
monthly_revenue
Out[17]:
Month Revenue
0 2022-03 98261.0
1 2022-04 27847245.0
2 2022-05 25326683.0
3 2022-06 22762217.0
In [18]:
Month = monthly_revenue['Month'].astype(str) # Convert Period to string for plotting 
Revenue = monthly_revenue['Revenue']
# Convert Period to string for plotting Revenue = monthly_revenue['Revenue'] # 
plt.plot(Month, Revenue, marker='o', color='r', label='Monthly Revenue')
plt.title("Revenue by Months")
plt.xlabel("Month") 
plt.ylabel("Revenue")
plt.xticks(rotation=45) 
plt.legend() 
plt.tight_layout() 
plt.show()
In [135]:
## Step 3: Weekly Revenue
In [6]:
sales['Week'] = sales['Date'].dt.to_period('W')

## Group by week
weekly_revenue = sales.groupby('Week')['Revenue'].sum().reset_index()
sns.lineplot(x='Week', y='Revenue', data= weekly_revenue, marker='o', color='orange')
plt.title("Weekly Revenue Trend")
plt.xlabel("Week")
plt.ylabel("Total Revenue")
plt.xticks(rotations=45)
plt.show()

In [ ]:
## 2: 2-Quantity Sold by Category
In [35]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))

# Group and sum quantities by category
cat_qty = sales.groupby("Category")["Qty"].sum()

# Plot bar chart
sns.barplot(
    x=cat_qty.index,
    y=cat_qty.values,
    palette="pastel"
)

plt.title("Quantity Sold by Category")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)   # ✅ Correct argument
plt.show()

In [52]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))

# Group and sum quantities by category
cat_qty = sales.groupby("Category")["Qty"].sum()

# Plot bar chart
sns.barplot(
    x=cat_qty.index,
    y=cat_qty.values,
    palette="pastel"
)

plt.title("Quantity Sold by Category")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)

# ✅ Add value labels on top of each bar
for i, val in enumerate(cat_qty.values):
    plt.text(i, val, str(val), ha='center', va='bottom', fontsize=10)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

In [ ]:
## Sales by Fulfilment Method
In [ ]:
plt.figure(figsize=(6,5))
full_sales = sales.groupby("Fulfilment")["Amount"].sum()

sns.barplot(
    x=full_sales.index,
    y=full_sales.values,
    palette = 'viridis'
)

plt.title("Sales by Fulfilment Method")
plt.show()
In [ ]:
plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))
size_sales = sales.groupby("Size")["Amount"].sum()

sns.barplot(
    x=size_sales.index,
    y=size_sales.values,
    palette="coolwarm")
plt.title("Sales by Size")
plt.show()

## COURIER STATUS DISTRIBUTION
In [8]:
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
sns.countplot(
    x="Courier Status",
    hue="Courier Status",  # Explicitly assign hue
    data=sales,
    palette="Accent",
    legend=False           # Optional: hides the legend if not needed
)
plt.title("Courier Status Distribution")
plt.xticks(rotation=30)
plt.show()
In [ ]:
 Sales Trend Over Time
In [15]:
sales['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(sales["Date"])
daily_sales = sales.groupby("Date")["Amount"].sum()


plt.figure(figsize = (12,5))
plt.plot(
    daily_sales.index,
    daily_sales.values,
    marker = "o")

plt.title("Sales Trend Over Time")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Total Sales")
plt.show()

Quantity Sold Over Time
In [16]:
daily_qty = sales.groupby("Date")["Qty"].sum()

plt.figure(figsize=(12,5))
plt.plot(daily_qty.index,
         daily_qty.values,
         marker = "o")

plt.title("Quantity Sold Over Time")
plt.show()

TOP 10 SKU by Revenue
In [6]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10,6))
sns.barplot(
    x=top_sku.values,
    y=top_sku.index,
    hue=top_sku.index,       # Explicitly assign hue to avoid FutureWarning
    palette="rocket",        # Stylish gradient palette
    legend=False             # Optional: hides legend since hue = y
)

plt.title("Top 10 SKUs by Revenue", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("Total Sales", fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel("SKU", fontsize=12)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

In [ ]:
B2B VS B2C Sales
In [8]:
b2b_sales = sales.groupby("B2B")["Amount"].sum()

plt.figure(figsize=(6,6))
plt.pie(
    b2b_sales,
    labels=["B2C", "B2B"],              # Proper labels
    autopct="%1.1f%%",
    startangle=90,
    colors=["#66c2a5", "#fc8d62"],      # Valid hex color codes
    explode=[0, 0.1]                    # Optional: highlights B2B slice
)

plt.title("B2B vs B2C Sales")
plt.show()
In [ ]:
Quantity by B2B
In [7]:
plt.figure(figsize=(6,5))
sns.barplot(
    x="B2B",
    y="Qty",
    hue="B2B",              # Explicitly assign hue
    data=sales,
    estimator=sum,
    palette="Set1",
    legend=False            # Optional: hides legend since hue = x
)

plt.title("Quantity Sold: B2B vs B2C", fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel("Segment", fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel("Total Quantity Sold", fontsize=12)
plt.xticks(ticks=[0,1], labels=["B2C", "B2B"])
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

In [19]:
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

Top5 = (sales.groupby("Category")["Qty"]
        .sum()
        .sort_values(ascending=False)
        .head(5)
        .reset_index())

plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))
ax = sns.barplot(x="Category", y="Qty", data=Top5, palette="Blues_d")
plt.title("Top 5 Product Categories by Quantity")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)

# Optional: Add data labels
for p in ax.patches:
    ax.annotate(f'{p.get_height():.0f}',
                (p.get_x() + p.get_width()/2., p.get_height()),
                ha='center', va='bottom')

plt.show()

In [50]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Clean column names (important if there are trailing spaces)
sales.columns = sales.columns.str.strip()

# Aggregate top 5 channels
top5 = (
    sales.groupby("Sales Channel")["Order ID"]
    .count()
    .sort_values(ascending=False)
    .head(5)
    .reset_index()
)

plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))
ax = sns.barplot(x="Sales Channel", y="Order ID", data=top5, palette="Blues_d")
plt.title("Top 5 Sales Channels by Order Count")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)

# Add data labels
for p in ax.patches:
    ax.annotate(f'{p.get_height():,.0f}',
                (p.get_x() + p.get_width()/2., p.get_height()),
                ha='center', va='bottom', fontsize=10)

plt.show()

In [70]:
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
sales["Date"] = pd.to_datetime(sales["Date"])

## Aggregate sales by Date
daily_Sales = sales.groupby("Date")["Amount"].sum().reset_index()

plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))
plt.plot(daily_Sales["Date"], daily_Sales["Amount"], marker="o")
plt.title("Daily sales Trend")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Total sales Trend")
plt.show()

In [9]:
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

sales['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(sales['Date'])
sales_trend = sales.groupby('Date')['Amount'].sum()

plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))
sales_trend.plot()
plt.title("Daily Sales Trend")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Total Sales Amount")
plt.show()

ax = sns.barplot(x=category_sales.index, y=category_sales.values)
plt.title("Sales by Category")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)


for container in ax.containers:
    ax.bar_label(container)
    
    

plt.show()
